key_insight: A truncated, smoothed prox-linear augmented Lagrangian framework can turn stationarity of a nonconvex minimax surrogate into a finite-time KKT certificate for constrained problems with convex-composite objectives and functional inequality constraints.
engineering_relevance: This gives optimization practitioners a principled way to solve difficult constrained nonconvex models in PDE-constrained and other engineering settings while controlling feasibility and multiplier blow-up.
method: Restrict dual variables to a compact set and solve a smoothed prox-linear augmented Lagrangian reformulation.
Use local conic regularity to show the artificial dual truncation becomes inactive near feasible iterates.
Derive explicit KKT residual rates: O(K^-1/3) with dual regularization and O(K^-1/2) under stronger local structural assumptions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical nonasymptotic analysis and explicit convergence rates, but no numerical experiments or empirical dataset are described.
watch_item: Performance depends on sufficiently large penalty parameters and local regularity/structural assumptions, so the guarantees may be sensitive to problem-specific verification of these conditions.
paper_title: Nonconvex Composite Functional Constraints via First-Order Augmented Lagrangian Methods under Local Regularity
